tick_over_tick_delta_vs_1200.interpretation: TWO pre-armed triggers converge cleanly on the same lever (min_confidence). (i) Trigger F (6th-BUY arm, RE-ARMED at 12:00 with action 0.62 → 0.65): SUNPHARMA at 12:14:33 is the 6th BUY of the session (90 min cadence). FIRES. (ii) Trigger N (BUY-without-intervening-SELL, RE-ARMED at 12:00 with action 0.62 → 0.65): SUNPHARMA BUY-fill at 12:14:33 came BEFORE the INDUSINDBK SELL-fill at 12:14:45 (12 sec gap, BUY-then-SELL inverse ordering — same pattern as the 11:45 TORNTPOWER/GRANULES sequence). FIRES. Both triggers point at min_confidence 0.65; one lever fire satisfies both (symmetric with the 12:00 simultaneous fire of max_sector_pct + min_confidence). Behavioral pattern of note: after the 11:45 GRANULES Pharma exit, Pharma re-enters at 12:14:33 (SUNPHARMA) and 12:14:55 (PHARMABEES) — a 29-minute SECTOR-LEVEL recycle, more aggressive than the TORNTPOWER 24h name-level recycle flagged at 12:00 under Trigger P. The strategy is now demonstrably 'trading old slots for new ones' at TWO timescales (sector and name). Risk level held at MEDIUM (not HIGH) because composite distress meta-trigger D is dormant on all 3 legs — book P&L is in fact MORE positive (+₹131.38 vs +₹89.58 at 12:00), no single-name distress, strategy not silent. STRUCTURAL IMPROVEMENT: BFSI grandfathered breach (22.45% > 22% cap) RESOLVED via INDUSINDBK exit — BFSI now 17.62%, 4.38 pp UNDER the cap, freeing future BFSI admissions if the 0.65 deployment cap relaxes. Cleanest single positive of the tick.

findings: SUNPHARMA BUY 30 @ ₹1734 = ₹52,020 (5.19% of capital) FILLED at 12:14:33 — the 6th BUY of the session and a NEW Pharma name. Pre-armed Trigger F (6th-BUY arm) FIRES on the spec-hard cadence threshold.

Trigger N (BUY-without-intervening-SELL, RE-ARMED at 12:00) FIRES cleanly: SUNPHARMA BUY-fill at 12:14:33 came BEFORE the INDUSINDBK SELL-fill at 12:14:45 (12-second gap, BUY-then-SELL ordering). Same inverse-of-discipline pattern as the 11:45 TORNTPOWER/GRANULES sequence flagged at 12:00.

PHARMABEES BUY 1500 @ ₹23.65 = ₹35,475 (3.55% of capital) submitted OPEN at 12:14:55 — the 7th BUY of the session and the 2nd Pharma name in 22 seconds. SECTOR-LEVEL RECYCLE PATTERN — more aggressive timescale than the TORNTPOWER 24h name-level recycle flagged at 12:00 under Trigger P.

BFSI grandfathered breach RESOLVED: INDUSINDBK SELL CLOSE at 12:14:45 (+₹41.80 realized) reduces BFSI cost-basis exposure from 22.45% to 17.62% — now 4.38 pp UNDER the 0.22 cap. Cleanest structural improvement of the tick. The 0.22 cap still binds against any aggressive BFSI re-stacking but no longer constrains organic positioning.

Deployment exposure: filled 54.28% → 59.45% (+5.17 pp), sitting 0.55 pp below the 60% Trigger M threshold; full-fill 68.53% → 72.44% (+3.91 pp), binding gap to 0.65 cap WIDENS to 7.44 pp (was 3.53 pp). Cap remains BINDING by design — no NEW BUY admissible without OPEN-order rejections or further SELLs.

Cash buffer: ₹406,119.48 (filled book, 40.55% of capital). On full fill (TATAPOWER + HINDZINC + PHARMABEES = ₹130,105): ₹276,014.48 (27.56%) — still comfortably above the 20% min_cash_buffer_pct. No cash-floor concern.
Daily realized P&L flips MORE positive: +₹89.58 → +₹131.38 (INDUSINDBK +₹41.80 win). Buffer to base 3% loss gate ~₹30,178; buffer to -1% findings trigger ~₹10,147. Loss-gate tightening absent.
Largest single-name (filled): NESTLEIND 11.99% — unchanged tick-over-tick, 3.01 pp BUFFER below the 0.15 per-name cap. No per-name trigger firing.

Behavioral pattern compounding: TWO sector-level recycles now visible — (i) TORNTPOWER name-level recycle (sold Day-3 10:12 → bought today 11:45, 24h cycle) under Trigger P; (ii) Pharma sector recycle (GRANULES exit 11:45:44 → SUNPHARMA + PHARMABEES re-entry 12:14:33–12:14:55, 29-min cycle). Combined with a 7-BUY / 3-SELL session ratio (2.33:1) under a binding 0.65 deployment cap, the strategy is plainly using SELL-then-BUY (or BUY-then-SELL) as a slot-rotation mechanism rather than as an absolute deployment-reduction mechanism. The 0.65 deployment cap, the 0.22 sector cap, and now the 0.65 min_confidence step are all gating WHAT comes in but not the ROTATION cadence itself.

actions_taken: Tightened min_confidence from 0.62 to 0.65 — fired TWO pre-armed triggers cleanly converging on the same lever: (i) Trigger F (6th-BUY arm) — SUNPHARMA at 12:14:33 is the 6th BUY of the session; (ii) Trigger N (BUY-without-intervening-SELL) — SUNPHARMA BUY-fill at 12:14:33 came BEFORE INDUSINDBK SELL-fill at 12:14:45 (12-sec BUY-then-SELL inverse ordering). Step calibrated +3 pp to 0.65 per spec — not overshoot since the trigger spec explicitly named 0.65 as the action target. n=5 closed-trade sample (3 wins, 2 losses, +₹1,556.38 net) is still small but the spec-hard pre-armed action is the disciplined call. Bite mechanics: the 0.65 deployed cap (binding 7.44 pp under full-fill projection) makes admission slots SCARCE; min_confidence 0.65 raises the quality bar on whatever qualifies. Non-punitive: applies to NEW admissions only; all 9 filled holdings and 3 in-flight OPEN orders grandfathered.

Risk level HELD at MEDIUM (not raised to HIGH or CRITICAL). Drivers held: (i) composite distress meta-trigger D dormant on all 3 legs — book P&L MORE positive at +₹131.38 realized (vs +₹89.58 at 12:00), no single-name distress data, strategy actively trading; (ii) Trigger I escalation legs (5th Power name OR Power >22%) both dormant — Power 19.77% holds, 4 names; (iii) the cadence/recycle pattern is being mechanically gated by the now-binding deployment cap, the 0.22 sector cap, and the new 0.65 confidence floor — system operating as designed.
